label: Escalate
report_text: PT-0042 experienced an increase in blood pressure to 190/110 mmHg approximately 12 hours after receiving Naxolimab. Patient reported a headache and nausea, leading to transport to the emergency room. In the ER, IV antihypertensives were administered, BP improved temporarily but then dropped to 80/50 mmHg. Patient remained unresponsive and passed away about 48 hours after the dose, with a potential connection to the study drug.
decision_memo: [ESCALATE — immediate] Grade 5 event detected - patient death reported. FDA MedWatch report required within 7 days. CTCAE grades (grader/Claude): {'hypertension': 4, 'headache': 3, 'nausea': 3, 'hypotension': 4, 'death': 5}. Cross-model check (verifier/gpt-4o-mini): {'hypertension': 4, 'headache': 3, 'nausea': 3, 'hypotension': 4, 'death': 5} — AGREEMENT.

label: Monitor
report_text: PT-0042, c/o headache starting 3 days post dose of Osurentib. BP 130/85, HR 72, VS stable. Labs: CBC within normal limits. Pt scales back on errands, reports difficulty concentrating on hobbies. Managed with 500 mg acetaminophen, study drug held one day.
decision_memo: [MONITOR — routine] Grade 2 event detected. Flag for next safety review meeting. CTCAE grades (grader/Claude): {'headache': 2}. Cross-model check (verifier/gpt-4o-mini): {'headache': 2} — AGREEMENT.

label: Dismiss
report_text: PT-0042. Fatigue noted on routine check, ~3 days post 2nd dose of Osurentib. VS: BP 120/78, HR 72, Temp 98.6°F, O2 Sat 98%. Pt c/o feeling tired, however, continues usual activities; no missed work. No treatment initiated, vitals remain within normal limits.
decision_memo: [DISMISS — none] Grade 1 event only. Continue monitoring per standard protocol. CTCAE grades (grader/Claude): {'fatigue': 1}. Cross-model check (verifier/gpt-4o-mini): {'fatigue': 1} — AGREEMENT.

label: Human review
report_text: Patient ID: PT-0042. Symptoms began approximately 5 hours after the administration of Veltorib. The patient presented with dyspnea, oxygen saturation at 85% on room air, and required admission to the hospital ward. Vital signs included a heart rate of 110 bpm, respiratory rate of 24 breaths/min, blood pressure of 100/60 mmHg, and a temperature of 99.5°F. The patient was unable to manage self-care tasks, demonstrated difficulty getting out of bed, and vomited twice during the evaluation, with a headache rated at 7/10. Intravenous fluids and oxygen therapy were administered during the hospital stay. The patient remained in the hospital and was unable to return to normal activities.
decision_memo: [ESCALATE — immediate] Multiple concurrent Grade 3 events detected. Immediate escalation per co-occurrence protocol. CTCAE grades (grader/Claude): {'dyspnea': 3, 'hypoxia': 3, 'vomiting': 3, 'headache': 3, 'hypotension': 3}. Cross-model check (verifier/gpt-4o-mini): {'dyspnea': 4, 'hypoxia': 3, 'vomiting': 3, 'headache': 3, 'hypotension': 3} — DISAGREEMENT. [FLAGGED FOR HUMAN REVIEW: grader/verifier grade disagreement]
